heading: How does this affect AWS (Amazon Web Services) VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) peering when I use custom DNS (Domain Name System)?
body:
Before 31 March 2020, applications deployed within AWS (Amazon Web Services) using custom
DNS (Domain Name System) services and VPC (Virtual Private Cloud)-peered with Atlas couldn't connect over
private IP addresses:

- Custom DNS (Domain Name System) resolved to public IP addresses.

- AWS (Amazon Web Services) internal DNS (Domain Name System) resolved to private IP addresses.

Applications deployed with custom DNS (Domain Name System) services in AWS (Amazon Web Services) should use
[Private IP for Peering connection strings](connstring-private).

To show these strings:
